Sisters (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $87,044,645Details
International Box Office $18,986,015
Worldwide Box Office $106,030,660
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,597,887
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,433,040
Total $12,030,927
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Sisters is the story of two disconnected sisters summoned home to clean out their childhood bedroom before their parents sell the family house. Looking to recapture their glory days, they throw one final high-school-style party for their classmates, which turns into the cathartic rager that a bunch of ground-down adults really need.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,922,855 (16.0% of total gross)
Legs:6.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:82.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,962 opening theaters/2,978 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $122,125,606

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 18th, 2015 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: December 18th, 2015 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
December 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Chile)
December 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Portugal)
December 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
December 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Slovenia)
Video Release: March 1st, 2016 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for crude sexual content and language throughout, and for drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2361, 2/18/2015)
Running Time: 117 minutes
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Parties Gone Wild, Sibling Rivalry, Single Parent, You Can't Go Home Again
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Universal Pictures, Little Stranger, Everyman Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Sisters is the story of two disconnected sisters summoned home to clean out their childhood bedroom before their parents sell the family house. Looking to recapture their glory days, they throw one final high-school-style party for their classmates, which turns into the cathartic rager that a bunch of ground-down adults really need.
